# M9 Watchdog Prerequisite Ordering Constraint

**Artifact ID**: `m9-watchdog-prerequisite-ordering`
**Generated by**: T38
**Schema version**: 1
**_non_authoritative**: true

## Constraint

Watchdog identity normalizers (Steps 31–37) **must** land before any later
watchdog/auditor consumer tests that consume shared watchdog files.  No
unordered concurrent edits are permitted across these shared files:

| Step | Module                           | Normalizes                          |
|------|----------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
| T31  | `watchdog/discovery.py`          | Exact worker identity tuples       |
| T32  | `watchdog/tmux_scan.py`          | Tmux/session correlation evidence  |
| T33  | `watchdog/signals.py`            | Heartbeat liveness from exact IDs  |
| T34  | `watchdog/orphans.py`            | Orphan/retry exact-identity tuples |
| T35  | `watchdog/repair_runner.py`      | Repair eligibility classification  |
| T36  | `tests/test_watchdog_cli.py`     | Watcher/log correlation fixtures   |
| T37  | `arnold_pipelines/megaplan/projection_drift.py` | Drift/lag metadata |

## Consumer Gate

Later watchdog/auditor consumer tests (T51, T54, T56, T57, T63) and
higher-level repair dispatch paths depend on the normalized identity types
and drift metadata produced by T31–T37.  These consumers must not attempt
unordered concurrent edits to `watchdog/discovery.py`, `watchdog/processes.py`,
`watchdog/signals.py`, `watchdog/orphans.py`, `watchdog/retry.py`,
`watchdog/repair_runner.py`, or `projection_drift.py` — all edits to these
files must flow through the identity-normalizer tasks first.

## Shared Files (no concurrent edits)

- `arnold_pipelines/megaplan/watchdog/discovery.py`
- `arnold_pipelines/megaplan/watchdog/processes.py`
- `arnold_pipelines/megaplan/watchdog/tmux_scan.py`
- `arnold_pipelines/megaplan/watchdog/registry.py`
- `arnold_pipelines/megaplan/watchdog/signals.py`
- `arnold_pipelines/megaplan/watchdog/snapshot.py`
- `arnold_pipelines/megaplan/watchdog/orphans.py`
- `arnold_pipelines/megaplan/watchdog/retry.py`
- `arnold_pipelines/megaplan/watchdog/repair_runner.py`
- `arnold_pipelines/megaplan/projection_drift.py`

## Enforcement

This ordering constraint is enforced structurally by the task dependency
graph (each consumer task declares explicit `depends_on` edges to the
normalizer tasks).  The critique-identified ordering correction is preserved
here as a review artifact; no new code behavior is introduced by this
document.
